# Forge Proving-Ground Spec

**Task:** 42.4-16
**Decision:** forge-11
**Date:** 2026-04-14
**Status:** spec (no harness exists yet)

## Purpose

This doc formalizes the measurable acceptance criteria for forge DOG-02 — "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from ETD's existing `REQUIREMENTS.md` via the editor's spawn flow." Decision forge-11 captures the why: ETD is the canonical proving-ground species because it has 12 historical generations to compare against, a clear binary pass state, and it lives in the existing eval framework so we aren't inventing a new test rig. DOG-01 (forge generates forge) is self-referential and prone to gaming; DOG-02 is externally verifiable against an artifact we didn't author for this purpose. This checklist is cited by forge `REQUIREMENTS.xml` DOG-02 and `ROADMAP.xml` phase 08 and must pass for at least one fresh generation produced by forge's spawn flow before forge phase 08 is considered complete.

## Proving-ground definition

Forge passes its proving-ground when it produces a generation of escape-the-dungeon, from ETD's existing `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/gad/REQUIREMENTS.md`, via forge's editor spawn flow (`/gad-eval-spawn` or editor equivalent per 42.4-13), that meets every item in the Observable Pass Criteria below. The generation must be spawned through a real `gad eval` worktree run — the orchestrator session MUST NOT hand-write code per decision forge-09.

## Observable pass criteria

Each item is a single observable check. Items marked `GAP:` have no existing infrastructure and need to be stood up as phase 08 scope; until then they are scored best-effort from RUN.md / SCORE.md notes.

- [ ] **PG-01 — Build artifact exists.** After spawn completes, a build artifact exists at `apps/portfolio/public/evals/escape-the-dungeon/<species>/v<N>/index.html` (via `gad eval preserve`).
  - How to verify: `gad eval verify` passes for the generated `(project, species, version)` tuple.

- [ ] **PG-02 — index.html loads cleanly.
... [truncated 5969 bytes] ...
 forge phase 08 is meant to close.

## Acceptance binding

This doc is cited as the acceptance criterion for forge `REQUIREMENTS.xml` DOG-02 and `ROADMAP.xml` phase 08 success criteria. When forge phase 08 is verified, every checkbox above (PG-01 through PG-09) must pass for at least one fresh generation produced by forge's spawn flow (`/gad-eval-spawn` or editor equivalent) against ETD's existing `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/gad/REQUIREMENTS.md`. Partial passes are logged but do not satisfy DOG-02.

## Open questions / gaps

- No Playwright (or any headless-browser) harness currently exists in the repo. Standing one up is phase 08 scope, not this doc's scope, and not task 42.4-16's scope.
- ETD has no canonical root selector, no canonical player test hook, and no canonical tile-count hook. The first forge-spawned generation that passes PG-04 will establish the probe; at that point we decide whether to add a "must expose `window.__game` test hook" line to ETD's `REQUIREMENTS.md` or to stick with screenshot-based probes.
- Input scheme (arrows vs WASD) varies across historical generations. PG-07's probe must try both and record which worked.
- The historical comparison table is scored from on-disk artifacts only. If we ever want real PG-02..PG-08 data for v7/v8/v9/v10 we'd have to re-run the probe against each preserved build; that's a separate backfill task, not a blocker for DOG-02.
- v8's SCORE.md "Build requirement gate PASSED" is the closest existing analogue to this checklist. That gate is a human-reviewed binary; forge's proving-ground replaces it with an automated 9-criterion checklist. Keeping both is fine — SCORE.md remains agent-reported self-scoring, forge-proving-ground is externally enforced.
- The historical record does not tell us whether any of v7..v10 would actually pass PG-07 (player moves on input). That is the single most load-bearing criterion and it is currently `?` for every historical generation. The first forge-spawned run that passes it sets the baseline.

    <content><![CDATA[
# app-forge — Project Overview

## What This Is

A local-first app and game generation studio. Captures user requirements through a structured intake UI, one-shots a greenfield generation from those requirements, and lets the user persist, fork, and lineage-trace generations. Each generation is a real worktree with files the user can edit through an embedded file-folder editor and an embedded terminal that runs Claude/Codex CLIs to spawn child generations ("broods"). Forge is itself the standard way to build apps in this monorepo — it dogfoods its own editor to build itself.

## Core Value

A user can describe what they want, get a one-shot generation, and then iterate on it through child generations that inherit code/content fr
... [truncated 5206 bytes] ...
ld generations — generations persist by design; user manages worktree lifecycle
- Centralized user account system — local-first, no accounts in v1

<task id="42.4-15" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape — readers should never manually combine them. Shipped: new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exporting `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics per decision gad-184: project defaults → inherits_from chain (deepest ancestor first) → target species; last write wins; arrays REPLACE not merge; species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) do not back-propagate to project; cycles throw. Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` to the loader and swapped `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` to use `loadResolvedSpecies` so the site sees the merged shape. Hermetic tests in `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` cover (a)-(e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + pure-function spot check (7/7 pass via `node --test`). Other legacy `gad.json` reads in bin/gad.cjs (eval run, eval verify, eval review, eval open/report) are orthogonal rename fallout and left for a separate surgical pass.</goal>
      <keywords>schema,inheritance,project-species,merge,loader,forge-10,gad-184</keywords>
    </task>

inputs.old_string:
<decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>
inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-183">
    <title>Proto-skills stage inside the owning project's `.planning/` and install outward into runtimes on demand</title>
    <summary>Proto-skills are not canonical framework assets yet; they are project-local planning artifacts awaiting human review. Policy: the canonical staging directory for proto-skills is `.planning/proto-skills/` inside the owning GAD project, not `skills/proto-skills/`. This keeps drafted proto-skills in the same lifecycle zone as tasks, decisions, snapshots, and workflows until the operator explicitly decides what to do next. From that staged location there are now two explicit outward moves: `gad evolution install <slug>` installs the staged proto-skill into one or more coding-agent runtimes for local testing while keeping it staged; `gad evolution promote <slug>` promotes the staged proto-skill into canonical `skills/` so it joins the framework DNA. Promotion must preserve provenance rather than dropping it.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Live evolution CLI paths move from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/`. (2) `gad evolution install` becomes the fast bridge from planning artifact to runtime test surface, mirroring the existing runtime install conventions (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`, local/global, optional config dir). (3) `gad evolution promote` now targets canonical `skills/` and carries the full staged bundle forward, including `PROVENANCE.md`. (4) Candidate generation and proto-skill docs must stop describing proto-skills as a subdirectory of the public skill catalog. (5) Historical candidate/eval artifacts may still mention the old path, but canonical source, CLI help, and planning docs should point at `.planning/proto-skills/`.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-184">
    <title>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract: merge-at-read-time eval loader</title>
    <summary>Every eval project has one `evals/<project>/project.json` declaring defaults (`techStack`, `co
... [truncated 552 bytes] ...
es`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(framework): project ⊇ species inheritance contract in eval loader (42.4-15)

Add lib/eval-loader.cjs — the canonical merger for evals/<project>/project.json
+ evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json. Exports loadProject,
loadSpecies, loadAllSpeciesRaw, loadResolvedSpecies, loadAllResolvedSpecies,
mergeProjectSpecies, resolveInheritanceChain.

Merge semantics (decision gad-184):
  1. project.json defaults are the base layer
  2. inherits_from chain walked deepest-ancestor first
  3. target species applied last, last write wins
  4. array fields REPLACE, not concat (no _extends convention yet)
  5. species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) stay local and
     never back-propagate to project defaults
  6. cycles throw a clear 'inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a' error

Wire bin/gad.cjs `eval list` through loadEvalProject + loadAllResolvedSpecies
instead of hand-reading gad.json (which no longer exists after 42.4-14). The
site prebuild scanEvalProjects path was already updated in a prior piggybacked
commit (32055ad) and is left as-is.

Hermetic tests at tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs cover all five
required cases (a-e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + mergeProjectSpecies purity
spot checks — 7/7 pass under `node --test`.

Updates TASK-REGISTRY 42.4-15 to done, STATE next-action, DECISIONS gad-184.

Other legacy gad.json reads inside bin/gad.cjs (eval run metadata, eval verify,
eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14
and left for a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep this
change scoped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

   ```

   ls apps/forge/.planning/
   ls <any other registered roots>
   ```

... [truncated 3096 bytes] ...
 projects init`."

Add a decision to `.planning/DECISIONS.xml` — next free id (check the latest; 42.4-15 allocated `gad-184`, so probably `gad-185` unless a sibling agent is also allocating). Title: "Canonical planning root shape". Body: minimum file set + health check binding + legacy list.

## Scope notes
- Another agent is working on **42.4-10** (sink/docs surface audit) in parallel. They touch `gad sink` / `gad docs` commands and will likely write a different references/ doc. Avoid touching those commands or writing a doc that overlaps their scope.
- Do NOT touch `gad projects init` — 42.4-08 owns it.
- Do NOT touch the eval loader or inheritance contract — 42.4-15 territory, already shipped.

## Verification

2. `references/project-shape.md` exists and has all six sections.
3. The audit matrix in the doc actually matches reality (spot-check one row).
4. DECISIONS.xml entry is well-formed.

## Commit
One commit in the submodule. Conventional prefix `docs(framework):` or `feat(framework):` — whichever matches the bulk of the change (if health check code is substantive, use `feat`). Reference 42.4-09 and the decision id. Do not push. Do not bump parent submodule pointer.

## Report back under 250 words
- Audit matrix summary: how many roots audited, how many are out-of-canonical-shape
- Canonical minimum file count (final list)
- Canonical optional file count
- Legacy items you flagged
- Decision id allocated
- `gad health` output sample (paste the new section)
- Any ambiguity you deferred

Be concrete. The audit is the value — an honest "here's what's actually on disk" grid is worth more than a polished definition with no grounding.

  <decision id="gad-180">
    <title>One canonical skill body per public skill name; wrappers and variants must not create duplicate identities in the canonical catalog</title>
    <summary>The current skill tree still contains cases where two different directories declare the same public skill identity (`name:` in frontmatter) with materially different bodies. That is a catalog bug, not healthy optionality: routing becomes ambiguous, install/catalog output becomes misleading, and humans cannot tell which body is authoritative. The upstream create-skill guidance already says "update, don't duplicate," and `merge-skill` exists specifically because overlap is a failure mode. Policy: the canonical `skills/` catalog gets exactly one authoritative body per public skill name. If two skills overlap, either (a) merge them into one canonical skill, (b) rewrite one so it is a distinct skill with a distinct public identity, or (c) archive the legacy body outside the canonical shipped catalog. Thin wrappers are allowed only when they have a distinct public identity and a distinct job (`create-proto-skill` vs a neutral upstream `create-skill` is valid; two different bodies both named `gad:plan-phase` is not). Tool/runtime-specific adapters belong in packaging/runtime layers, not as duplicate skill identities.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Shipping/catalog filters that already prefer `gad-*` over same-name legacy aliases are a stopgap, not the final architecture. (2) Follow-on cleanup should physically move same-name legacy bodies out of canonical `skills/` into an archive/docs lineage area or merge them into the `gad-*` canonical versions. (3) Skill authoring remains free to use GAD-specific structure, references, and parsing conventions, but the externally parsed contract stays minimal and portable: valid YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, optional status/provenance fields) plus a stable SKILL.md shape. <impact>(1) Adopt a naming convention that makes artifact type visually obvious: `skills/gad-<command>/`, `workflows/gad-<command>.md`, `references/<topic>.md`, `templates/<topic>.*`. (2) When consolidating duplicate or legacy skill content, salvage ordered procedure into the workflow file and salvage explanatory prose into references; do not keep two SKILL.md bodies for the same public command. (3) Validation should fail if canonical `skills/` contains duplicate public `name:` values or a plain-name alias for a canonical `gad-*` command skill.   <decision id="gad-182">
    <title>Canonical framework assets need an automated integrity gate: unique public skill names and resolvable `@...` references</title>
    <summary>Manual grep passes are not enough to keep the framework surface coherent after repeated structural migrations. The canonical asset tree now has enough cross-linking (`skills/` → `workflows/` / `references/` / `templates/`, agent prompts, hook docs, site catalog generation) that two failure modes need to be enforced automatically: (1) duplicate public skill identities in canonical `skills/`, and (2) broken canonical alias references such as `@workflows/...` or `@references/...` inside framework assets. Policy: ship and maintain a repository-level validator that scans the canonical asset roots, fails on duplicate public `name:` values, and fails on broken alias references. Warnings for non-canonical or historical/generated surfaces are acceptable, but canonical roots must stay clean.</summary>
    <impact>(1) `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` becomes the fast integrity check for framework shape migrations. (2) New command surface: `node scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` and package alias `check:assets`. (3) Future cleanups should pre
... [truncated 2914 bytes] ...
`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

# Canonical Planning Root Shape

> **Status:** normative. Source of truth for `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08)
> and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, the "gad health" check).
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-09, decision gad-185).

## 1. Purpose

Every GAD-tracked project lives under a *planning root* — a directory containing
a `.planning/` subdirectory populated with a known set of XML planning files and
optional supporting directories. Over the 40+ phases of framework evolution the
"known set" has drifted: some projects have `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, some have
`phases/`, some have `plans/`, some have `proto-skills/`, some have `notes/`,
and no single file names the canonical shape. `gad projects init` scaffolds a
4-file markdown set that nobody actually uses, while `gad projects audit` only
checks `STATE.xml` + `ROADMAP.xml` as required and treats `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`DECISIONS.xml`, and `AGENTS.md` as interchangeable "recommended" files. This
drift surfaced concretely on 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding: `gad
projects init` wrote markdown templates that had to be hand-converted to XML,
and nothing caught the shape mismatch until a human noticed (see
`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`).

This document pins the canonical minimum file set, the canonical optional file
set, the legacy/deprecated names to avoid, and the minimum valid XML header for
each required file. It is the contract both `gad projects init` and `gad
projects audit` MUST agree on.

## 2. Canonical minimum file set

Every `.planning/` directory MUST contain all of the following files. A planning
root that is missing any of these is "out of canonical shape" and `gad projects
audit` will flag it.

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STATE.xml` | Current phase, status, and next-action for the project. |
| `ROADMAP.xml` | Ordered phases + milestones. |
| `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` | Task ledger (every task with status, skill, agent, type). |
| `DECISIONS.xml` | Architecture / policy decision log. |

These four f
... [truncated 8948 bytes] ...
| gad | global | app-forge | magicborn | grime-time | mb-cli | repub-builder |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `phases/` | Y | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `plans/` | Y | - | Y | - | - | - | - |
| `proto-skills/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `workflows/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `notes/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `specs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `context-frameworks/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `docs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `archive/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `codebase/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `debug/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `reports/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `sessions/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `templates/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `todos/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `quick/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `skills/` (legacy) | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |

### 6d. Out-of-canonical-shape flags

- **`grime-time`** — has legacy project-local `skills/` directory (decision
  gad-183 deprecated this in favor of `.planning/proto-skills/`). Remediation:
  move or archive. Otherwise canonical-minimum-clean.
- **`gad` (framework) and `global`** — have legacy `config.json` inside
  `.planning/`. Remediation: confirm no current CLI path reads it, then remove.
- **`magicborn`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`** — missing both optional
  `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. Not a shape violation (both
  are optional), but flagged as "thin" — these projects may benefit from
  capturing scope and negative results explicitly. Informational only.
- No root contains `PROJECT.xml`. Consistent with its optional status in §3.
- No root contains a legacy markdown `*.md` variant of the canonical XML set.
  The hand-converted forge scaffolding is the only known recent drift and has
  already been corrected.

## 7. Change log

| Date | Task | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-14 | 42.4-09 | Initial version. Defined minimum (4 files), optional files + dirs, legacy list, per-file headers, and audit matrix. Decision gad-185. |

inputs.content:
# Sink MD → XML Conversion Audit (task 42.4-10)

## 1. Question

Does any existing `gad` command convert human-authored markdown planning prose
(`PROJECT.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `STATE.md`) into the canonical
XML planning files (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) that the rest of the framework reads?
Short answer: **no**. The sink surface is MD/XML-tolerant on the way *out* to
MDX, XML-only on the way *in* from MDX, and nothing in the tree parses MD
prose into the canonical XML shape. Details below.

## 2. Command surface audited

Read directly from `bin/gad.cjs` and `lib/docs-compiler.cjs` (not help text).

- **`gad docs compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1650`) — iterates configured roots, calls
  the same `compileDocs` path as `gad sink compile` under the hood. Planning
  root → MDX in `docs_sink`. One-way, source → sink.
- **`gad docs list`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1709`) — read-only enumeration of planning
  files, DOCS-MAP entries, and sink feature-docs. Does not transform.
- **`gad sink status`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7052`) — stat-based comparison between
  each `SINK_SOURCE_MAP` entry in `.planning/` and the compiled MDX. Reports
  `missing | stale | human-authored | ok`. No writes.
- **`gad sink diff`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7094`) — calls `diffSink()` in
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs`, produces a unified diff between what `compile`
  *would* write and what's on disk in the sink. No writes.
- **`gad sink compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7132`) — delegates to
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs:compile()`. For each `SOURCE_MAP` row it picks the
  first existing file among `[STATE.xml, STATE.md]`, etc. If the hit is `.xml`
  it runs `xmlToMdBody()` (dedicated per-file converters: `stateXmlToMd`,
  `roadmapXmlToMd`, `decisionsXmlToMd`, `taskRegistryXmlToMd`,
  `requirementsXmlToMd`, `errorsXmlToMd`, `blockersXmlToMd`). If the hit is
  `.md` it just calls `stripFrontmatter()` and emits the body verbatim wrapped
  in MDX frontmatter. **Nothing parses the MD into XML.**
- **`gad sink syn
... [truncated 7040 bytes] ...
0
LOC "extend decompile" budget the task goal floated — which is further
evidence this should be a new subcommand, not an extension. Do **not**
implement in 42.4-10; this is a spec for a future focused task.

Open design question for the follow-up:
- Do we want hydrate to be bidirectional-safe (i.e. after hydrate, can you
  `migrate-schema` back without loss)? Only relevant if the MD is
  round-trippable.
- Does `gad projects init` (42.4-08) want to call hydrate as a final step so
  markdown scaffolding users can get XML for free? Probably yes.

## 7. Related tasks

- **42.4-08** (`gad projects init`) — will benefit from hydrate when a user
  scaffolds a new project from MD templates. Not blocking, can wait.
- **42.4-09** (canonical project shape audit) — parallel; establishes which
  files *must* exist. Hydrate produces exactly that set.
- **42.4-11** (sink-pipeline coherence doc) — will cite this audit as the
  reference for "what MD→XML gap exists" and reference the hydrate spec as
  the planned fix. This doc stays narrow on the conversion gap; 42.4-11 owns
  the full pipeline map.

## Surprises from the audit

1. `sink decompile` is misnamed. It does not decompile. It creates empty
   shells. If a new developer read the help text they would expect it to read
   MDX and emit XML — it does neither. Worth a rename or a doc fix regardless
   of whether hydrate ships. (Not in scope for this task, flag for 42.4-11.)
2. `sink compile` silently accepts MD input and degrades to pass-through.
   There's no warning that "this MD file won't be queryable by `gad state` /
   `gad tasks` / etc." — the user just gets MDX in the sink and silence from
   the readers. A `gad health` warning (42.4-09's territory) for "MD present,
   XML missing" would be a cheap win.
3. `migrate-schema` archives XML after conversion. Running it against the
   decompile-generated empty stubs actively destroyed them (archived to
   `archive/xml/`). If anyone ever extends `decompile` to do real work, that
   interaction is a footgun.

  `migrate-schema` back without loss)? Only relevant if the MD is
  round-trippable.
- Does `gad projects init` (42.4-08) want to call hydrate as a final step so
  markdown scaffolding users can get XML for free? Probably yes.

## 7. Related tasks

- **42.4-08** (`gad projects init`) — will benefit from hydrate when a user
  scaffolds a new project from MD templates. Not blocking, can wait.
- **42.4-09** (canonical project shape audit) — parallel; establishes which
  files *must* exist. Hydrate produces exactly that set.
- **42.4-11** (sink-pipeline coherence doc) — will cite this audit as the
  reference for "what MD→XML gap exists" and reference the hydrate spec as
  the planned fix. This doc stays narrow on the conversion gap; 42.4-11 owns
  the full pipeline map.

## Surprises from the audit

1. `sink decompile` is misnamed. It does not decompile. It creates empty
   shells. If a new developer read the help text they would expect it to read
   MDX and emit XML — it does neither. Worth a rename or a doc fix regardless
   of whether hydrate ships. (Not in scope for this task, flag for 42.4-11.)
2. `sink compile` silently accepts MD input and degrades to pass-through.
   There's no warning that "this MD file won't be queryable by `gad state` /
   `gad tasks` / etc." — the user just gets MDX in the sink and silence from
   the readers. A `gad health` warning (42.4-09's territory) for "MD present,
   XML missing" would be a cheap win.
3. `migrate-schema` archives XML after conversion. Running it against the
   decompile-generated empty stubs actively destroyed them (archived to
   `archive/xml/`). If anyone ever extends `decompile` to do real work, that
   interaction is a footgun.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/DECISIONS.xml","content":"    <title>Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract: merge-at-read-time eval loader</title>
    <summary>Every eval project has one `evals/<project>/project.json` declaring defaults (`techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent`, domain/description/rubric metadata) and one `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json` per species. Species may override any project field and also carry species-only fields (`inherits_from`, `dna`, species-specific `description`). Readers — CLI (`gad eval list/run/preserve`), the site prebuild (`build-site-data.mjs`), and the forge editor — MUST NOT hand-merge project + species. Policy: all access goes through `lib/eval-loader.cjs` which exposes `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, and `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics are: (1) start from project.json as base; (2) walk `inherits_from` deepest-ancestor first, applying each species on top; (3) apply target species last with last-write-wins; (4) array fields REPLACE, never concat (a future `_extends` convention could opt into merging but is out of scope); (5) species-only fields stay on the resolved shape and never back-propagate to the shared project defaults; (6) cycles throw a clear `inherits_from cycle detected: a -> b -> a` error; (7) unknown parent references throw. Cross-references forge-10 which captured the editor-UX rationale (separate "edit project" vs "edit species" surfaces demand a single canonical merger).</summary>
    <impact>(1) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` is the canonical location for project-species composition; adding or changing merge rules is one file, one function. (2) `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` now route through the loader, replacing hand-rolled project.json + species.json combinations. (3) Hermetic inheritance tests live at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` and lock the contract (5 required cases + 2 smoke checks, all green via `node --test`). (4) The forge editor can safely present "edit project settings" vs "edit species settings" as separate surfaces: whatever the operator edits at each level round-trips through the same merge on reload. (5) Other legacy `gad.json` reads inside `bin/gad.cjs` (eval run metadata, eval verify, eval review rubric, eval report) are orthogonal rename fallout from 42.4-14 and should migrate to the loader in a follow-up surgical pass — not refactored here to keep 42.4-15 scoped. (6) Arrays replacing (not concatenating) is a load-bearing choice: callers who want to "extend" a project-level skill list must spell out the full species list explicitly until an `_extends` convention is designed.</impact>
  </decision></decisions>

</decision>
  <decision id="gad-185">
    <title>Canonical planning-root shape: minimum file set, optional set, legacy list, and audit binding</title>
    <summary>Every GAD planning root (`[[planning.roots]]` in `gad-config.toml`) has a canonical shape. The canonical MINIMUM file set that every `.planning/` directory MUST contain is four files: `STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`. This is the "everyone can run `gad snapshot`/`state`/`tasks`/`decisions` against it" bar and is present in 7/7 audited roots. The canonical OPTIONAL file set is `REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`, `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, `BLOCKERS.xml`, `PROJECT.xml` (reserved for future identity-file standardization — currently unused by any root), `DOCS-MAP.xml`, `AGENTS.md`, `CONVENTIONS.md`, `README.md`, plus the directories `phases/`, `plans/`, `proto-skills/`, `workflows/`, `notes/`, `specs/`, `todos/`, `debug/`, `reports/`, `sessions/`, `codebase/`, `docs/`, `archive/`, `templates/`, `context-frameworks/`, `quick/`, and transient `.eval-runs/`. The LEGACY/deprecated list that `gad projects audit` should warn on: `gad.json` (→ `species.json`, phase 43 / 42.4-14), `PROJECT.md`/`STATE.md`/`ROADMAP.md`/`DECISIONS.md`/`REQUIREMENTS.md` (→ canonical XML equivalents), `config.json` inside `.planning/` (superseded by repo-root `gad-config.toml`), `REPOPLANNER-TO-GAD-MIGRATION-GAPS.md` (one-shot migration note), and project-local `skills/` as a proto-skill staging dir (→ `.planning/proto-skills/` per decision gad-183). The definition lives at `vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-shape.md` with per-file minimum XML headers, the full audit matrix across all seven registered roots, and flags for out-of-canonical shape (grime-time has legacy `skills/`; `gad`/`global` have legacy `config.json`; magicborn/mb-cli/repub-builder are "thin" — missing optional `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` but still canonical-minimum-clean). That references doc is the contract that both `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08, not yet shipped) and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, this task) agree on.</summary>
    <impact>(1) `bin/gad.cjs projects audit` now computes a canonical-shape report per root — new helper `computeCanonicalShape(planDir)` plus three additive checks (`canonical_minimum`, `canonical_optional`, `canonical_legacy`) per root and a new "Canonical planning-root shape" summary block under the existing table, each citing `references/project-shape.md` as the source of truth. Legacy required/recommended checks are kept untouched for backwards compatibility. (2) Task 42.4-08 (`gad projects init` canonical-XML scaffold) now has a concrete contract to implement: the six per-file minimum XML headers in §5 of the references doc are what it MUST write, and the four-file minimum in §2 is what it MUST produce on every invocation regardless of format flag. (3) `PROJECT.xml` is NOT in the canonical minimum — it is present in 0/7 audited roots, so promoting it would immediately flag every project as out-of-canonical. It is left in the optional list with a "reserved for a future phase" note; any future decision that wants to require it must also ship the backfill. (4) The audit command is additive, not a refactor: existing JSON consumers see three new `check` entries per project but no renames or removals. (5) Follow-up cleanup candidates surfaced by the audit: grime-time legacy `skills/` directory, framework + global legacy `config.json`, and three "thin" roots that would benefit from capturing `REQUIREMENTS.xml` / `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. None are blocking; all are informational.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\references\project-shape.md","content":"# Canonical Planning Root Shape

> **Status:** normative. Source of truth for `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08)
> and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, the "gad health" check).
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-09, decision gad-185).

## 1. Purpose

Every GAD-tracked project lives under a *planning root* — a directory containing
a `.planning/` subdirectory populated with a known set of XML planning files and
optional supporting directories. Over the 40+ phases of framework evolution the
"known set" has drifted: some projects have `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, some have
`phases/`, some have `plans/`, some have `proto-skills/`, some have `notes/`,
and no single file names the canonical shape. `gad projects init` scaffolds a
4-file markdown set that nobody actually uses, while `gad projects audit` only
checks `STATE.xml` + `ROADMAP.xml` as required and treats `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`DECISIONS.xml`, and `AGENTS.md` as interchangeable "recommended" files. This
drift surfaced concretely on 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding: `gad
projects init` wrote markdown templates that had to be hand-converted to XML,
and nothing caught the shape mismatch until a human noticed (see
`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`).

This document pins the canonical minimum file set, the canonical optional file
set, the legacy/deprecated names to avoid, and the minimum valid XML header for
each required file. It is the contract both `gad projects init` and `gad
projects audit` MUST agree on.

## 2. Canonical minimum file set

Every `.planning/` directory MUST contain all of the following files. A planning
root that is missing any of these is "out of canonical shape" and `gad projects
audit` will flag it.

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STATE.xml` | Current phase, status, and next-action for the project. |
| `ROADMAP
... [truncated 9496 bytes] ...
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `phases/` | Y | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `plans/` | Y | - | Y | - | - | - | - |
| `proto-skills/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `workflows/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `notes/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `specs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `context-frameworks/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `docs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `archive/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `codebase/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `debug/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `reports/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `sessions/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `templates/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `todos/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `quick/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `skills/` (legacy) | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |

### 6d. Out-of-canonical-shape flags

- **`grime-time`** — has legacy project-local `skills/` directory (decision
  gad-183 deprecated this in favor of `.planning/proto-skills/`). Remediation:
  move or archive. Otherwise canonical-minimum-clean.
- **`gad` (framework) and `global`** — have legacy `config.json` inside
  `.planning/`. Remediation: confirm no current CLI path reads it, then remove.
- **`magicborn`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`** — missing both optional
  `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. Not a shape violation (both
  are optional), but flagged as "thin" — these projects may benefit from
  capturing scope and negative results explicitly. Informational only.
- No root contains `PROJECT.xml`. Consistent with its optional status in §3.
- No root contains a legacy markdown `*.md` variant of the canonical XML set.
  The hand-converted forge scaffolding is the only known recent drift and has
  already been corrected.

## 7. Change log

| Date | Task | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-14 | 42.4-09 | Initial version. Defined minimum (4 files), optional files + dirs, legacy list, per-file headers, and audit matrix. Decision gad-185. |

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\references\sink-md-xml-audit.md","content":"# Sink MD → XML Conversion Audit (task 42.4-10)

## 1. Question

Does any existing `gad` command convert human-authored markdown planning prose
(`PROJECT.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `STATE.md`) into the canonical
XML planning files (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) that the rest of the framework reads?
Short answer: **no**. The sink surface is MD/XML-tolerant on the way *out* to
MDX, XML-only on the way *in* from MDX, and nothing in the tree parses MD
prose into the canonical XML shape. Details below.

## 2. Command surface audited

Read directly from `bin/gad.cjs` and `lib/docs-compiler.cjs` (not help text).

- **`gad docs compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1650`) — iterates configured roots, calls
  the same `compileDocs` path as `gad sink compile` under the hood. Planning
  root → MDX in `docs_sink`. One-way, source → sink.
- **`gad docs list`** (`bin/gad.cjs:1709`) — read-only enumeration of planning
  files, DOCS-MAP entries, and sink feature-docs. Does not transform.
- **`gad sink status`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7052`) — stat-based comparison between
  each `SINK_SOURCE_MAP` entry in `.planning/` and the compiled MDX. Reports
  `missing | stale | human-authored | ok`. No writes.
- **`gad sink diff`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7094`) — calls `diffSink()` in
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs`, produces a unified diff between what `compile`
  *would* write and what's on disk in the sink. No writes.
- **`gad sink compile`** (`bin/gad.cjs:7132`) — delegates to
  `lib/docs-compiler.cjs:compile()`. For each `SOURCE_MAP` row it picks the
  first existing file among `[STATE.xml, STATE.md]`, etc. If the hit is `.xml`
  it runs `xmlToMdBody()` (dedicated per-file converters: `stateXmlToMd`,
  `roadmapXmlToMd`, `decisionsXmlToMd`, `taskRegistryXmlToMd`,
  `requirementsXmlToMd`, `errorsXmlToMd`, `b
... [truncated 7482 bytes] ...
ould be a new subcommand, not an extension. Do **not**
implement in 42.4-10; this is a spec for a future focused task.

Open design question for the follow-up:
- Do we want hydrate to be bidirectional-safe (i.e. after hydrate, can you
  `migrate-schema` back without loss)? Only relevant if the MD is
  round-trippable.
- Does `gad projects init` (42.4-08) want to call hydrate as a final step so
  markdown scaffolding users can get XML for free? Probably yes.

## 7. Related tasks

- **42.4-08** (`gad projects init`) — will benefit from hydrate when a user
  scaffolds a new project from MD templates. Not blocking, can wait.
- **42.4-09** (canonical project shape audit) — parallel; establishes which
  files *must* exist. Hydrate produces exactly that set.
- **42.4-11** (sink-pipeline coherence doc) — will cite this audit as the
  reference for "what MD→XML gap exists" and reference the hydrate spec as
  the planned fix. This doc stays narrow on the conversion gap; 42.4-11 owns
  the full pipeline map.

## Surprises from the audit

1. `sink decompile` is misnamed. It does not decompile. It creates empty
   shells. If a new developer read the help text they would expect it to read
   MDX and emit XML — it does neither. Worth a rename or a doc fix regardless
   of whether hydrate ships. (Not in scope for this task, flag for 42.4-11.)
2. `sink compile` silently accepts MD input and degrades to pass-through.
   There's no warning that "this MD file won't be queryable by `gad state` /
   `gad tasks` / etc." — the user just gets MDX in the sink and silence from
   the readers. A `gad health` warning (42.4-09's territory) for "MD present,
   XML missing" would be a cheap win.
3. `migrate-schema` archives XML after conversion. Running it against the
   decompile-generated empty stubs actively destroyed them (archived to
   `archive/xml/`). If anyone ever extends `decompile` to do real work, that
   interaction is a footgun.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\references\project-shape.md","content":"# Canonical Planning Root Shape

> **Status:** normative. Source of truth for `gad projects init` (task 42.4-08)
> and `gad projects audit` (task 42.4-09, the "gad health" check).
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-09, decision gad-185).

## 1. Purpose

Every GAD-tracked project lives under a *planning root* — a directory containing
a `.planning/` subdirectory populated with a known set of XML planning files and
optional supporting directories. Over the 40+ phases of framework evolution the
"known set" has drifted: some projects have `HUMAN-TODOS.xml`, some have
`phases/`, some have `plans/`, some have `proto-skills/`, some have `notes/`,
and no single file names the canonical shape. `gad projects init` scaffolds a
4-file markdown set that nobody actually uses, while `gad projects audit` only
checks `STATE.xml` + `ROADMAP.xml` as required and treats `REQUIREMENTS.xml`,
`DECISIONS.xml`, and `AGENTS.md` as interchangeable "recommended" files. This
drift surfaced concretely on 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding: `gad
projects init` wrote markdown templates that had to be hand-converted to XML,
and nothing caught the shape mismatch until a human noticed (see
`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`).

This document pins the canonical minimum file set, the canonical optional file
set, the legacy/deprecated names to avoid, and the minimum valid XML header for
each required file. It is the contract both `gad projects init` and `gad
projects audit` MUST agree on.

## 2. Canonical minimum file set

Every `.planning/` directory MUST contain all of the following files. A planning
root that is missing any of these is "out of canonical shape" and `gad projects
audit` will flag it.

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `STATE.xml` | Current phase, status, and next-action for the project. |
| `ROADMAP
... [truncated 9496 bytes] ...
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `phases/` | Y | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `plans/` | Y | - | Y | - | - | - | - |
| `proto-skills/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `workflows/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `notes/` | Y | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `specs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `context-frameworks/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `docs/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `archive/` | Y | - | - | - | - | - | - |
| `codebase/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `debug/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `reports/` | - | Y | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `sessions/` | - | Y | - | - | - | - | - |
| `templates/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `todos/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `quick/` | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |
| `skills/` (legacy) | - | - | - | - | Y | - | - |

### 6d. Out-of-canonical-shape flags

- **`grime-time`** — has legacy project-local `skills/` directory (decision
  gad-183 deprecated this in favor of `.planning/proto-skills/`). Remediation:
  move or archive. Otherwise canonical-minimum-clean.
- **`gad` (framework) and `global`** — have legacy `config.json` inside
  `.planning/`. Remediation: confirm no current CLI path reads it, then remove.
- **`magicborn`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`** — missing both optional
  `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`. Not a shape violation (both
  are optional), but flagged as "thin" — these projects may benefit from
  capturing scope and negative results explicitly. Informational only.
- No root contains `PROJECT.xml`. Consistent with its optional status in §3.
- No root contains a legacy markdown `*.md` variant of the canonical XML set.
  The hand-converted forge scaffolding is the only known recent drift and has
  already been corrected.

## 7. Change log

| Date | Task | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-14 | 42.4-09 | Initial version. Defined minimum (4 files), optional files + dirs, legacy list, per-file headers, and audit matrix. Decision gad-185. |

</context>

Initialize a new project through unified flow: questioning → research (optional) → requirements → roadmap.

**Creates:**
- `.planning/PROJECT.md` — project context
- `gad-config.toml` — canonical workflow preferences
- `.planning/config.json` — generated compatibility mirror
- `.planning/research/` — domain research (optional)
- `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — scoped requirements
- `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — phase structure
- `.planning/STATE.md` — project memory

**After this command:** Run `/gad:plan-phase 1` to start execution.
</objective>

</execution_context>

Preserve all workflow gates (validation, approvals, commits, routing).

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\skills\gad-plan-phase\SKILL.md","content":"---
name: gad:plan-phase
description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop
argument-hint: "[phase] [--auto] [--research] [--skip-research] [--gaps] [--skip-verify] [--prd <file>] [--reviews] [--text]"
agent: gad-planner
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - Task
  - WebFetch
  - mcp__context7__*
---
<objective>
Create executable phase prompts (PLAN.md files) for a roadmap phase with integrated research and verification.

**Default flow:** Research (if needed) → Plan → Verify → Done

**Orchestrator role:** Parse arguments, validate phase, research domain (unless skipped), spawn gad-planner, verify with gad-plan-checker, iterate until pass or max iterations, present results.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-plan-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase number: $ARGUMENTS (optional — auto-detects next unplanned phase if omitted)

**Flags:**
- `--research` — Force re-research even if RESEARCH.md exists
- `--skip-research` — Skip research, go straight to planning
- `--gaps` — Gap closure mode (reads VERIFICATION.md, skips research)
- `--skip-verify` — Skip verification loop
- `--prd <file>` — Use a PRD/acceptance criteria file instead of discuss-phase. Parses requirements into CONTEXT.md automatically. Skips discuss-phase entirely.
- `--reviews` — Replan incorporating cross-AI review feedback from REVIEWS.md (produced by `/gad:review`)
- `--text` — Use plain-text numbered lists instead of TUI menus (required for `/rc` remote sessions)

Normalize phase input in step 2 before any directory lookups.
</context>

<process>
Execute the plan-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-plan-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (validation, research, planning, verification loop, routing).
</process>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\skills\gad-execute-phase\SKILL.md","content":"---
name: gad:execute-phase
description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization
argument-hint: "<phase-number> [--wave N] [--gaps-only] [--interactive]"
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Edit
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - Bash
  - Task
  - TodoWrite
  - AskUserQuestion
---
<objective>
Execute all plans in a phase using wave-based parallel execution.

Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context and handles its own plan.

Optional wave filter:
- `--wave N` executes only Wave `N` for pacing, quota management, or staged rollout
- phase verification/completion still only happens when no incomplete plans remain after the selected wave finishes

Flag handling rule:
- The optional flags documented below are available behaviors, not implied active behaviors
- A flag is active only when its literal token appears in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If a documented flag is absent from `$ARGUMENTS`, treat it as inactive

Context budget: ~15% orchestrator, 100% fresh per subagent.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-execute-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase: $ARGUMENTS

**Available optional flags (documentation only — not automatically active):**
- `--wave N` — Execute only Wave `N` in the phase. Use when you want to pace execution or stay inside usage limits.
- `--gaps-only` — Execute only gap closure plans (plans with `gap_closure: true` in frontmatter). Use after verify-work creates fix plans.
- `--interactive` — Execute plans sequentially inline (no subagents) with user checkpoints between tasks. Lower token usage, pair-programming style. Best for small phases, bug fixes, and verification gaps.

**Active flags must be derived from `$ARGUMENTS`:**
- `--wave N` is active only if the literal `--wave` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--gaps-only` is active only if the literal `--gaps-only` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--interactive` is active only if the literal `--interactive` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If none of these tokens appear, run the standard full-phase execution flow with no flag-specific filtering
- Do not infer that a flag is active just because it is documented in this prompt

Context files are resolved inside the workflow via `gad-tools init execute-phase` and per-subagent `<files_to_read>` blocks.
</context>

<process>
Execute the execute-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-execute-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (wave execution, checkpoint handling, verification, state updates, routing).
</process>

inputs.content:
# Sink Pipeline — Coherence Map

> **Status:** normative. Pipeline map for the full `.planning/` → docs sink → site flow.
> **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-14 (task 42.4-11).
> **Companion docs:** `references/sink-md-xml-audit.md` (per-command behavior),
> `references/project-shape.md` (canonical file set).

## 1. Purpose

This doc is the **coherence map** for GAD's planning compilation pipeline. It
answers "which surface do I edit when?" at the granularity of a single command
per concrete task. It complements `sink-md-xml-audit.md` (which catalogs what
each `gad sink` / `gad docs` command *actually does*, including the broken
parts) and `project-shape.md` (which pins the canonical `.planning/` file set).
Intended reader: an agent who just ran `gad snapshot` and now needs to change
planning state without accidentally editing a derived artifact.

## 2. Source of truth

**XML files under `.planning/` are authoritative. Everything else is derived.**
MDX in `docs/<id>/planning/` is a compiled view for humans and the site. Static
sites under `dist/site/` (from `gad site compile`) are a further derived
surface. There is no supported reverse flow: `sink-md-xml-audit.md`
determination **(C)** establishes that no command parses MDX or MD prose back
into canonical XML — `sink decompile` only creates empty shells, and
`migrate-schema` goes the wrong direction (XML→MD). If you edit an MDX file
in the sink, the next `gad sink compile` overwrites it.

## 3. The three registries

All three live in `gad-config.toml` at the repo root.

| Registry | Declares | Consumed by |
|---|---|---|
| `[[planning.roots]]` | `.planning/` dirs across the monorepo (one per project) | `gad snapshot`, `gad state`, `gad tasks`, `gad phases`, `gad decisions`, `gad workspace show`, `gad projects audit` |
| `[[docs.projects]]` | Non-planning-root feature-doc projects (apps, platforms, lore) that compile into `docs_sink/<id>/` | `gad docs list`, `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile` (iteration target) |
| `[[evals.roots]]` | Eval project roots (a
... [truncated 4729 bytes] ...
le` is misnamed.** Help text implies reverse compilation; it
  actually only creates empty XML shells when source is absent. It never reads
  MDX. Rename or doc-fix pending.
- **`sink compile` silently degrades on MD input.** If it finds `STATE.md`
  instead of `STATE.xml` it pass-throughs the body into MDX without warning
  that none of the readers (`gad state`, `gad tasks`, …) can query that
  content. A health warning is a cheap win (42.4-09 territory).
- **`migrate-schema` goes XML→MD and archives the XML.** Running it against a
  planning root that has decompile-generated stub XMLs actively destroys them.
  Wrong direction for almost every current use case.
- **`sink compile` and `site compile` read `.planning/` on separate axes.**
  The site does NOT consume the MDX sink — it reads XML directly. Editing MDX
  in the sink does not change what `gad site compile` produces.
- **No MD→XML path exists.** A markdown-authored planning root is a dead-end
  for the readers until a human hand-converts it. See `sink-md-xml-audit.md`
  §6 for the `gad planning hydrate` spec (not implemented).
- **`docs_sink` is singular.** There is exactly one sink path for the whole
  monorepo. Per-root override is not supported; every planning root compiles
  into the same tree keyed by root id.

## 7. Related docs

- `references/sink-md-xml-audit.md` — per-command behavior audit; the "why
  decompile doesn't decompile" reference. Source for §6 footguns.
- `references/project-shape.md` — canonical `.planning/` file set + per-file
  minimum valid XML headers. Contract for `gad projects init` and `gad
  projects audit`.
- `references/planning-config.md` — `gad-config.toml` schema reference.
- `.planning/workflows/gad-new-project.md` — full new-project flow that
  consumes this pipeline.

## 8. Change log

| Date | Task | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-14 | 42.4-11 | Initial version. Pipeline map, three-registry table, quick-reference table (19 rows), footgun list, flow diagram. Cites 42.4-10's audit as dependency. |

Currently it writes 4 markdown template files (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) containing literal template placeholders. The rest of the monorepo uses 6+ canonical XML files (STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml, REQUIREMENTS.xml). Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding — the markdown output had to be hand-converted to XML, see ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. Fix: update `bin/gad.cjs projects init` (or wherever the scaffold lives) to write the canonical XML file set with valid empty headers. Optional `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path. Also scaffold the missing files (TASK-REGISTRY, DECISIONS, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS) regardless of format.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,projects-init,scaffold,xml,canonical-format</keywords>

inputs.new_string:
<task id="42.4-08" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>`gad projects init` defaults to canonical XML and scaffolds the full file set. Currently it writes 4 markdown template files (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) containing literal template placeholders. The rest of the monorepo uses 6+ canonical XML files (STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml, REQUIREMENTS.xml). Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding — the markdown output had to be hand-converted to XML, see ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. Fix: update `bin/gad.cjs projects init` (or wherever the scaffold lives) to write the canonical XML file set with valid empty headers. Optional `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path. Also scaffold the missing files (TASK-REGISTRY, DECISIONS, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS) regardless of format.</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-14. Rewrote `projectsInit` in `bin/gad.cjs` against the contract in `references/project-shape.md` §5 (decision gad-185). Default format is now `xml` and scaffolds six files: the canonical minimum (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) plus the two strongly-recommended optionals called out in the task goal (`REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`). Each template is a literal string from project-shape.md §5 with project id and today's date substituted — no `{{placeholder}}` tokens leak. `STATE.xml` seeds `status=active`, empty `current-phase`, and `next-action="Project initialized. Define requirements."`; `ROADMAP.xml` seeds a single `phase id="00" status="planned"`; `TASK-REGISTRY`/`DECISIONS`/`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS` are empty roots; `REQUIREMENTS.xml` includes a TODO comment. Added `--format md` flag that restores the legacy markdown scaffold but still writes `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` / `DECISIONS.xml` / `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` so the resulting root is canonical-minimum-clean regardless of format. Added `--projectid` arg (falls back to slug of `--name`). Added a pre-flight overwrite check: if any target file already exists, refuse with the blocker list unless `--force` is passed. Preserved the existing `gad-config.toml` root-registration behavior. Added a "Next steps" hint pointing at `gad projects audit`, `gad discuss-phase`, and `gad plan-phase`. Verification: `gad projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"` in a temp dir produced the six XML files, all parse under `fast-xml-parser`, canonical_minimum check is 4/4; re-run without `--force` was refused with the six-file blocker list; re-run with `--force` overwrote cleanly; `--format md` in a fresh temp dir produced the four legacy markdown files plus the three XML ledgers. Incident that prompted this task: ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. No new decision — `gad-185` frames the canonical shape and remains the authoritative reference for the init contract.</resolution>
      <commands>
        <command>node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"</command>
        <command>node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs projects init --projectid test-md --name "Test MD" --format md</command>
      </commands>
      <keywords>cli,projects-init,scaffold,xml,canonical-format,gad-185</keywords>
    </task>

name: gad:plan-phase
description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop
argument-hint: "[phase] [--auto] [--research] [--skip-research] [--gaps] [--skip-verify] [--prd <file>] [--reviews] [--text]"
agent: gad-planner
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - Task
  - WebFetch
  - mcp__context7__*
---
<objective>
Create executable phase prompts (PLAN.md files) for a roadmap phase with integrated research and verification.

**Default flow:** Research (if needed) → Plan → Verify → Done

**Orchestrator role:** Parse arguments, validate phase, research domain (unless skipped), spawn gad-planner, verify with gad-plan-checker, iterate until pass or max iterations, present results.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-plan-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase number: $ARGUMENTS (optional — auto-detects next unplanned phase if omitted)

**Flags:**
- `--research` — Force re-research even if RESEARCH.md exists
- `--skip-research` — Skip research, go straight to planning
- `--gaps` — Gap closure mode (reads VERIFICATION.md, skips research)
- `--skip-verify` — Skip verification loop
- `--prd <file>` — Use a PRD/acceptance criteria file instead of discuss-phase. Parses requirements into CONTEXT.md automatically. Skips discuss-phase entirely.
- `--reviews` — Replan incorporating cross-AI review feedback from REVIEWS.md (produced by `/gad:review`)
- `--text` — Use plain-text numbered lists instead of TUI menus (required for `/rc` remote sessions)

Normalize phase input in step 2 before any directory lookups.
</context>

<process>
Execute the plan-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-plan-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (validation, research, planning, verification loop, routing).
</process>

name: gad:execute-phase
description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization
argument-hint: "<phase-number> [--wave N] [--gaps-only] [--interactive]"
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Edit
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - Bash
  - Task
  - TodoWrite
  - AskUserQuestion
---
<objective>
Execute all plans in a phase using wave-based parallel execution.

Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context and handles its own plan.

Optional wave filter:
- `--wave N` executes only Wave `N` for pacing, quota management, or staged rollout
- phase verification/completion still only happens when no incomplete plans remain after the selected wave finishes

Flag handling rule:
- The optional flags documented below are available behaviors, not implied active behaviors
- A flag is active only when its literal token appears in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If a documented flag is absent from `$ARGUMENTS`, treat it as inactive

Context budget: ~15% orchestrator, 100% fresh per subagent.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@workflows/gad-execute-phase.md
@references/ui-brand.md
<
... [truncated 187 bytes] ...
. Use when you want to pace execution or stay inside usage limits.
- `--gaps-only` — Execute only gap closure plans (plans with `gap_closure: true` in frontmatter). Use after verify-work creates fix plans.
- `--interactive` — Execute plans sequentially inline (no subagents) with user checkpoints between tasks. Lower token usage, pair-programming style. Best for small phases, bug fixes, and verification gaps.

**Active flags must be derived from `$ARGUMENTS`:**
- `--wave N` is active only if the literal `--wave` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--gaps-only` is active only if the literal `--gaps-only` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- `--interactive` is active only if the literal `--interactive` token is present in `$ARGUMENTS`
- If none of these tokens appear, run the standard full-phase execution flow with no flag-specific filtering
- Do not infer that a flag is active just because it is documented in this prompt

Context files are resolved inside the workflow via `gad-tools init execute-phase` and per-subagent `<files_to_read>` blocks.
</context>

<process>
Execute the execute-phase workflow from @workflows/gad-execute-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (wave execution, checkpoint handling, verification, state updates, routing).
</process>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\TASK-REGISTRY.xml","content":"      </commands>
      <keywords>schema,project-shape,canonical-format,health,audit,references,gad-185</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="42.4-10" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="general-purpose-subagent"><goal>Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdown→XML conversion gap before adding new commands. CORRECTION 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. User pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` — bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the MD→XML conversion needed. Audit: does any existing command convert markdown PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/ROADMAP/STATE prose into canonical XML? If yes, document it and close this task. If no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. Don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,sink,docs,convert,md-to-xml,audit,decompile</keywords>
      <resolution>Determination (C) — no existing command handles it. `sink compile` reads MD but passes it through into MDX without structuring; `sink decompile` creates empty XML shells (name is misleading, it does not read sink content); `migrate-schema` goes XML→MD, opposite direction. Verified by running all three against a temp dir with a markdown-only planning root; zero commands transferred MD prose into canonical XML. Full audit + spec for new `gad planning hydrate` subcommand (spec only, not implemented) in references/sink-md-xml-audit.md. Extending `sink decompile` was rejected because its documented invariant "never overwrites existing source files" conflicts with the useful MD→XML case. Follow-up task needed to implement hydrate (~150 LOC).</resolution>
    </task>

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(framework): default gad projects init to canonical XML scaffold (42.4-08)

Rewrites `gad projects init` against references/project-shape.md §5
(decision gad-185). Default --format is xml and writes the six-file
canonical set:

  STATE.xml / ROADMAP.xml / TASK-REGISTRY.xml / DECISIONS.xml  (minimum)
  REQUIREMENTS.xml / ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml                   (strongly recommended)

Each template substitutes real project id and today's date — no literal
{{placeholder}} tokens. STATE.xml seeds status=active and next-action
"Project initialized. Define requirements."; ROADMAP.xml seeds
phase id=00 status=planned; TASK-REGISTRY/DECISIONS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS
are empty roots; REQUIREMENTS has a TODO comment.

Also adds:

- `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path (still writes the
  three canonical XML ledgers so the resulting root is audit-clean
  regardless of format)
- `--projectid` arg (falls back to slug of --name)
- Pre-flight overwrite check with `--force` escape hatch
- Post-init next-steps hint pointing at projects audit / discuss-phase
  / plan-phase

Preserves existing gad-config.toml root registration behavior. No new
decision — gad-185 remains the authoritative contract. Incident that
prompted this: ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log -1 --oneline

<task id="44-01" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>/project-market becomes a real multi-project launcher inside the vendor site. Today /project-market is a single static page; it must list every registered GAD planning root (read from `gad-config.toml` [[planning.roots]] + [[evals.roots]]), render one card per project with species count, latest generation per species, and a "launch eval" affordance. The launch affordance routes to a dev-only handler that invokes `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` via the phase-44.5 command bridge (or a stub that prints the command when the bridge isn't present yet). Any GAD-based project that vendors this site inherits the same surface automatically — the site is "about the project it is launched from" and its children. First cut: static card grid driven by build-site-data reading the workspace roots; launch button is wired to a placeholder route until 44.5-02 lands. Captures the user's constraint that site-visible management is a hard requirement for any non-eval loop work.</goal>
      <keywords>project-market,launcher,workspace-roots,multi-project,site</keywords>
    </task>
  </phase>
inputs.new_string:
<task id="44-01" type="site" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>/project-market ProjectCard gains a dev-only Launch eval button that actually runs `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` via a Node child_process spawn from the dev server and streams stdout/stderr back to the browser. POST /api/dev/launch-eval refuses unless NODE_ENV=development. First cut is the live run path; legacy planning-root migration and app-forge build-preservation are split into separate tasks.</goal>
      <keywords>project-market,launcher,child-process,site,dev-bridge</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-02" type="cleanup" status="planned"><goal>Migrate legacy planning roots (grime-time-site, repub-builder, mb-cli-framework, magicborn, global) from planning-only shape into the canonical eval-project shape under `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json` so they appear on /project-market with launch affordance. Per user alignment 2026-04-14: "every project IS an eval project — no separate non-eval card shape." For each root: (1) decide canonical species (likely just one — the project itself), (2) write project.json + species.json with minimum fields (id, name, description, domain, techStack, contextFramework=gad), (3) register under [[evals.roots]] in gad-config.toml if not already, (4) verify it shows up in `gad eval list` and on /project-market. Does not require porting existing planning files — legacy .planning/ trees stay where they are; the eval shape is a thin overlay for launcher purposes.</goal>
      <keywords>migration,eval-shape,planning-roots,project-market</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="44-03" type="pipeline" status="planned"><goal>App-forge build preservation: today `gad eval verify` flags app-forge v1 MISSING because preservation expects a statically-servable dist/ but forge is a Next.js app producing `.next/` (server components, no output:export). Decide the path: (a) add `output: "export"` to `apps/forge/next.config.mjs` and rewrite any server-only code so `next build` produces `out/`, then teach `gad eval preserve` to copy `out/` for Next.js eval species; (b) skip static preservation for forge entirely and instead preserve a headless Playwright trace / screenshot bundle as the "build artifact"; (c) run forge as a live dev server from the portfolio site when viewing its eval (no static artifact). Capture the decision, update `gad eval preserve` per the chosen path, re-run preservation for app-forge v1, and confirm `gad eval verify` goes green. Also file a runtime-identity record so the preservation audit's other MISSING column clears.</goal>
      <keywords>eval-preservation,next-js,app-forge,gad-eval-verify</keywords>
    </task>
  </phase>

inputs.new_string: <next-action>44-01 promoted from stub to live child_process bridge 2026-04-14. `site/app/api/dev/launch-eval/route.ts` now runs `node bin/gad.cjs eval run --project <id> --execute` via `node:child_process.spawn` from the repo root and streams stdout/stderr/exit back as SSE. `ProjectCard.tsx` parses the event-stream, renders stdout (green) / stderr (red) / meta (grey) lines in a scrollable log panel under the card, tracks running/done/err states, and exposes an Abort button that signals the server via AbortController → SIGTERM to the child. -- grime-time-site --
# Grime Time (`grime_time_site`)

Exterior-cleaning marketing site and admin, based on the official Payload **[with-vercel-website](https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates/with-vercel-website)** template (Next.js App Router, [layout builder](https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/tree/main/templates/with-vercel-website#layout-builder), SEO, forms, drafts).

**Planning & workflows:** [`.planning/`](.planning/) (RepoPlanner). **Roadmap / phases:** [`.planning/ROADMAP.xml`](.planning/ROADMAP.xml), [`.planning/phases/`](.planning/phases/). **CRM:** EngageBay — [`.planning/workflows/crm-and-integrations.md`](.planning/workflows/crm-and-integrations.md), checklist [`.planning/workflows/engagebay-integration-review.md`](.planning/workflows/engagebay-integration-review.md). **Lead process (staff, in portal):** sign in as admin → **Docs** → *Lead to customer runbook* ([`src/content/docs/lead-to-customer-runbook.md`](src/content/docs/lead-to-customer-runbook.md)). **Admin GitHub login:** [`.planning/workflows/supabase-github-admin-auth.md`](.planning/workflows/supabase-github-admin-auth.md). **Content & seeding:** [`.planning/workflows/content-and-seeding.md`](.planning/workflows/content-and-seeding.md). **UX backlog (portal / admin / quotes):** [`.planning/workflows/admin-portal-quotes-ux.md`](.planning/workflows/admin-portal-quotes-ux.md).
-- repub-builder --
# @portfolio/repub-builder

Build **RichEPub** (`.repub`) files from a Vite + React + Tailwind project, and reuse the same reader/runtime package in host apps. The output is a ZIP container with a manifest (`repub.json`), entry HTML, and bundled assets.

## Install
-- mb-cli-framework --
# @magicborn/mb-cli-framework

Shared helpers for **Magicborn operator CLIs** (`magicborn`, vendor CLIs, …): completion tokens, vendor scope, TTY/plain mode, CLI theme tokens, and an optional **terminal chat** built with [assistant-ui for Ink](https://www.assistant-ui.com/docs/ink).

## Install

  ids across roots.

  instead of fs.readdirSync(EVALS_DIR).

matches `gad eval list` output. Typecheck clean.

marketplace polish pass.

"app-forge/gad") so POSTing projectId=project.id routed the CLI to
`gad eval run --project app-forge/gad`, which errored with "project
not found". Handler now resolves `project.project ?? id.split("/")[0]`.

placeholder-image stand-in. Featured star and species badge move onto
the hero with backdrop blur; run/playable counts appear as a corner

contextFramework (purple) + workflow, deduplicated so we don't repeat
gad/gad/gad. Card body switches to flex col for consistent alignment.

the user raised — today the catalog is prebuild-static and eval runs
between dev-server restarts go invisible on the grid until the next
predev rebuild. Proposed path: GET /api/dev/projects in dev mode that
re-runs the 44-02 multi-root scanner on demand, with production mode
keeping the static catalog.

Typecheck clean. Launch endpoint reprobed with projectId=app-forge,
streamed `Eval run: app-forge v2 (execute mode)` as expected.
